blueprint_refs: Motivation for a target day is computed from the past 7 observed days (not necessarily consecutive calendar days)
validation targets must have >=7 prior observed days ... (not necessarily consecutive calendar days)

evidence.metrics: stop_count_abs_mean_error=0.158, stop_count_kl=0.129, tod_jsd_avg=0.0255; code returns default motivation unless ALL of (t-1..t-7) exist.

motivation-window-uses-consecutive-calen.error_identification: Motivation conditioning is frequently skipped unless the prior 7 calendar days are all present; this contradicts the spec’s “past 7 observed days” requirement and weakens day-specific stop-count and timing modulation.
motivation-window-uses-consecutive-calen.root_cause_analysis: In MobilitySimulator._compute_motivation, it constructs days = [target_day-1 ... target_day-7] and bails out to neutral motivation if any are missing. Blueprint requires selecting the most recent 7 observed days prior to the target date (not necessarily consecutive).
motivation-window-uses-consecutive-calen.correct_approach: Replace the consecutive-day check with: collect all observed dates < target_day from agent.observed_2021, take the latest 7 of those, and compute features from them. If <7 exist, either exclude the day (as validation split already does) or fall back to a weaker motivation estimate (e.g., use all available prior days up to 7). Ensure no future leakage by strictly using obs_d < target_day.
motivation-window-uses-consecutive-calen.key_insight: When a spec says “last N observed events,” never implement it as “last N calendar days”—select by available observations to avoid silent feature dropout.

blueprint_refs: Parse ... into trajectories (date, ordered visits with POI category, POI id, time)

evidence.metrics: transition_divergence=0.0251 (could be artificially low if ordering is normalized); sample outputs show many repeated identical POIs with small time gaps.

trajectory-parsing-loses-sequence-order-.error_identification: The parser re-sorts visits by time (and stable index), which can destroy the original listed order and collapse/alter adjacent transition structure when multiple events share identical timestamps or when the string order matters.
trajectory-parsing-loses-sequence-order-.root_cause_analysis: parse_day_record builds visits via regex iteration (which preserves textual order), then explicitly sorts by (time_min, original_index). Blueprint requires ordered sequences; for ties, the string order is the authoritative order. Re-sorting also changes training-derived transitions and dwell computations that assume adjacency from the observed sequence.
trajectory-parsing-loses-sequence-order-.correct_approach: Preserve the textual order from VISIT_RE.finditer and only enforce monotonic time during simulation/serialization. If you must handle out-of-order timestamps, prefer a minimal repair: keep order, and clamp non-monotone times to last_t (similar to serialize_day_record) while logging a counter of repaired times.
trajectory-parsing-loses-sequence-order-.key_insight: For sequence models, keep the dataset’s native ordering; do not “normalize” ordering unless the spec explicitly defines a canonical order.

evidence.metrics: trip_distance_wasserstein=3.174 (largest error among reported metrics); sample outputs show many long, erratic trips and frequent returns to the same anchor-like POI.

poi-choice-utility-double-penalizes-dist.error_identification: Trip distance distribution mismatch remains relatively high, suggesting the spatial utility may be mis-specified and over-regularized toward home/anchor in ways that distort inter-stop distances.
poi-choice-utility-double-penalizes-dist.root_cause_analysis: In _choose_next_poi, anchor is set to home_poi or current_poi, and the utility penalizes both distance from current_poi (alpha_dist) and distance from home_poi (alpha_anchor) simultaneously on every step. If home_poi is also the start/current frequently, this effectively double-counts proximity to home and can distort the distribution of consecutive-stop distances (especially when “home” is included as a stop).
poi-choice-utility-double-penalizes-dist.correct_approach: Follow the blueprint’s intended meaning of anchor: use home/work as an anchor depending on time-of-day/state (e.g., daytime anchor=work if known; otherwise home), and avoid applying both penalties when anchor==current_poi (skip anchor term or treat anchor as a separate fixed target). Additionally, compute travel-time and trip-distance from consecutive non-identical POIs to avoid home-inflated short hops if required by evaluation.
poi-choice-utility-double-penalizes-dist.key_insight: When a utility has multiple distance terms, guard against accidental correlation/double-counting (e.g., current==anchor) to avoid skewing spatial distributions.

blueprint_refs: sample baseline number of stops ... from learned per-user distributions (Pattern)

evidence.metrics: stop_count_abs_mean_error=0.158 and stop_count_kl=0.129; stop counts match mean fairly well but distribution still diverges.

stop-count-sampling-distorts-distributio.error_identification: Stop-count mean error is small but KL divergence is still noticeable, indicating distribution shape mismatch (e.g., too peaky or too heavy-tailed).
stop-count-sampling-distorts-distributio.root_cause_analysis: _sample_stop_count builds a categorical distribution from the empirical histogram, then adjusts log-probabilities using math.log(mult) * (c / baseline_mean). This is an ad-hoc tilt that can over-amplify high counts for mult>1 and suppress them for mult<1, changing distribution shape more than intended (especially when baseline_mean is small).
stop-count-sampling-distorts-distributio.correct_approach: Use a distributional modulation that preserves the empirical shape better: (a) sample from empirical histogram unmodified, then apply a bounded adjustment (e.g., +/-1 stop with some probability based on motivation), or (b) fit a parametric count model per user (e.g., Poisson/NegBin) and modulate its mean with motivation. Keep min/max feasibility constraints, but avoid log-tilting proportional to count unless calibrated.
stop-count-sampling-distorts-distributio.key_insight: Match both mean and distribution: avoid heuristic reweighting that can silently warp tails; prefer interpretable count models or small post-sampling adjustments.

blueprint_refs: trip distance distributions ... join lat/lon via poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude.json

evidence.metrics: trip_distance_wasserstein=3.174; meta shows gt coord_match_rate=0.301 while sim coord_match_rate=0.618 (different missingness).

evaluation-trip-distance-coverage-mismat.error_identification: Ground-truth and simulated trip-distance distributions are computed over different subsets of transitions due to different coordinate match rates, which can bias Wasserstein comparisons and inflate/deflate the metric unrelated to behavioral quality.
evaluation-trip-distance-coverage-mismat.root_cause_analysis: The simulator explicitly prioritizes POIs with coordinates (candidates_for_category and _choose_next_poi reorder pools toward has_coords). Meanwhile, many ground-truth POIs lack coordinates (gt match rate ~0.30). Evaluator._trip_distances drops transitions without coordinates, so the compared distributions are on mismatched supports.
evaluation-trip-distance-coverage-mismat.correct_approach: Align evaluation support: when computing trip distances, restrict both GT and SIM to transitions where BOTH endpoints have coordinates in the shared POI catalog (or alternatively, impute missing coords consistently for both). If you keep the current metric definition, remove the simulator’s coordinate-prioritization bias (or make it match the observed coordinate availability by category/user) to reduce selection effects.
evaluation-trip-distance-coverage-mismat.key_insight: Distance metrics are sensitive to missing geo joins; ensure GT/SIM are evaluated on the same eligible set or apply consistent imputation, otherwise the metric confounds behavior with data coverage.

blueprint_refs: OUTPUT FORMAT STRICT: serialize each simulated day as a single string ... with no extra text/JSON/markdown.

evidence.metrics: Execution succeeded without calling LLM; these classes are dead code unrelated to mobility simulation.

non-mobility-llm-review-agents-unused-an.error_identification: The code includes an OpenAI-based review-writing subsystem that is unrelated to the mobility simulator and not used in the pipeline, increasing complexity and risking environment failures if activated.
non-mobility-llm-review-agents-unused-an.root_cause_analysis: Top-of-file LLM integration and ReviewAuthor stack do not participate in main(). This is off-blueprint and could introduce dependency/credential issues if inadvertently called.
non-mobility-llm-review-agents-unused-an.correct_approach: Remove unused LLM/review-agent code paths from this mobility simulator module. If LLM is intended for Persona inference (optional per blueprint), implement it in a bounded, off-by-default component that consumes only pre-target 2021 data and never affects output format.
non-mobility-llm-review-agents-unused-an.key_insight: Keep the simulation artifact minimal: remove dead/orthogonal subsystems to reduce failure surface and maintain spec focus.
